question_text: For the reaction $\text{N}_2(g) + 3\text{H}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NH}_3(g)$, the equilibrium concentrations are $[\text{N}_2] = 0.50\,\text{M}$, $[\text{H}_2] = 0.30\,\text{M}$, and $[\text{NH}_3] = 0.20\,\text{M}$. Calculate $K_c$ and determine which direction the reaction shifts if $[\text{H}_2]$ is increased to $0.60\,\text{M}$.

steps.description: Write the equilibrium expression for Kc
steps.explanation: Products over reactants — what are the exponents?
steps.reinforcement: Good — products over reactants with stoichiometric coefficients as exponents.

steps.description: Substitute the equilibrium concentrations and calculate Kc
steps.explanation: Plug in the values — what's 0.30 cubed?
steps.tutor_speech: Plug in the concentrations and calculate.

steps.description: Calculate the reaction quotient Q with the new H2 concentration
steps.explanation: Same formula as Kc, but with the new concentrations. What changes?
steps.reinforcement: Q = 0.370 — now you can compare it to Kc.
steps.tutor_speech: Calculate Q with the new hydrogen concentration.

steps.description: Compare Q to Kc and determine the direction of shift
steps.explanation: Is Q less than, equal to, or greater than Kc? What does Le Chatelier's principle say?
steps.reinforcement: Exactly — Q < Kc means too few products, so the reaction shifts right to make more NH3.
steps.tutor_speech: Compare Q to Kc and state the shift direction.
